text: Layer normalization normalizes data across features for each sample independently.
Consider this input matrix with batch samples and feature dimensions.
For each sample, we compute the mean across all its features.
Next, we calculate the variance to measure how spread out the features are.
Each feature is then normalized by subtracting the mean and dividing by standard deviation.
We add a small epsilon value for numerical stability.
This produces normalized outputs with zero mean and unit variance per sample.
